questions.text: Operational decisions within defined parameters are made by the appropriate team member without my involvement or approval.
There is a documented framework that defines which decisions can be made at each level of the organisation without escalation to me.
When I am unavailable for more than 48 hours, the organisation continues to make decisions at its normal pace without accumulating a backlog requiring my attention on return.
My leadership team has the structural authority to make decisions that commit the organisation to significant operational, financial, or strategic actions within defined parameters.
Decisions made by my leadership team in my absence are not routinely revisited, reversed, or re-approved when I return.
The organisation has a defined process for making decisions in my absence that does not require my remote involvement.

The knowledge required to serve our key clients — their history, requirements, preferences, and relationship context — is documented and accessible to the appropriate team members without reference to me.
Our operational processes are documented to a standard that would allow a competent new team member to execute them without requiring my guidance or institutional memory.

The organisation's strategic context — why key decisions were made, what alternatives were considered, and what the current strategic priorities are — is documented and accessible to the leadership team.
Supplier relationships, commercial terms, and negotiation history are documented and held by the organisation rather than managed personally by me.
The organisation has a systematic process for capturing, documenting, and distributing knowledge as it is created — rather than relying on individuals to hold it.
If I were to leave the organisation tomorrow, the leadership team would have access to all the information required to continue operating without a significant knowledge gap.
Our most significant client relationships are managed by the organisation through a defined relationship structure — not primarily through my personal involvement.
Key clients would continue their relationship with the organisation if I were no longer their primary point of contact.
Our organisation's authority and credibility with key external stakeholders — clients, partners, investors — is held institutionally, not dependent on my personal presence or reputation.
Strategic partnerships and commercial agreements are structured so that they are held by the organisation rather than contingent on my personal involvement.
My leadership team has the authority and credibility to represent the organisation in senior external conversations — with clients, investors, or partners — without my presence.
The organisation's sales and business development process does not depend on my personal involvement or reputation to generate new client relationships.
The organisation has operated at full capacity for a period of 30 or more consecutive days without my active involvement in operational decisions.
There is a documented operational continuity plan that defines how the organisation functions if I am absent for an extended period.
The organisation's financial operations — including payment approvals, banking authorities, and financial reporting — can be managed without my involvement within defined parameters.
Our operational rhythm — team meetings, reporting cycles, performance reviews, and project governance — continues without disruption when I am not present.
The organisation has a defined leadership hierarchy that holds operational authority in my absence — with clear accountability for decisions and outcomes.
If I were incapacitated for 90 days, the organisation would continue to operate, serve its clients, and meet its obligations without structural failure.
My organisation has a leadership layer — below me — with clearly defined roles, authorities, and accountabilities that are documented and understood.
Each member of my leadership team has a defined domain of authority within which they can make decisions, commit resources, and be held accountable for outcomes — without reference to me.
My leadership team operates as a functional governance body — with a defined meeting structure, decision-making protocol, and collective accountability — not simply as a group of direct reports.
There is a defined succession of authority within my leadership team — a clear understanding of who holds organisational authority if I am absent or unavailable.
My leadership team has demonstrated the ability to resolve significant operational or strategic challenges without escalating to me for a decision.
The organisation would retain its leadership capability and structural integrity if one or two members of my leadership team were to leave simultaneously.
